rulings.Case Title: JULIO CASTILLO VS. ALBERTSON'S COMPANIES, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-15-2026. LINE 1. PLAINTIFF JULIO CASTILLO'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Julio Castillo's motion to quash deposition subpoenas is granted and his request for monetary sanctions is granted in small part. Although Safeway may have good cause to have non-expert discovery reopened to allow for the service of the subpoenas and production of records responsive to the subpoenas, non-expert discovery closed prior to the issuance of each of the four subpoenas. Safeway's request in its opposition papers to file a motion to reopen fact discovery on shortened time must be made by an ex parte application and is not cognizable based on opposition papers alone. Because Safeway acted without substantial justification in issuing the subpoenas, Castillo is entitled to an award of sanctions. Safeway and its counsel must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $600 to Castillo no later than April 30, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JASPREET KALRA ET AL VS. THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA; ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-15-2026. LINE 9. PLAINTIFFS JASPREET KALRA, and NAVDEEP SINGH'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs' request to compel the production of the performance reviews, competency evaluations, disciplinary records, and training records in the personnel file of Dr. Ransohoff is granted. All these records must be produced to plaintiffs with a verification that all have been produced and none withheld no later than April 30, 2026. Plaintiffs may only use the records for this case and may not disseminate any of the records to anyone other than their experts without agreement of the Regents or court order and must destroy the records when the case has concluded. The records sought by this remaining portion of plaintiffs' motion to compel are all directly relevant and the Regents have not identified any particularized reason why any of the records deserve greater privacy protection than other confidential records. The Regents may not withhold any of the records on the grounds of EvidenceCode 1157 since their counsel stated at the last hearing that none of the records were protected by that statute.. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied since the Regents did not act without substantial justification. Counsel for the plaintiffs must prepare an order reflecting all of the rulings on this motion including the rulings made at the last hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: SIDA WANG FAGGIOLI ET AL VS. JAGUAR LAND ROVER NORTH AMERICA, LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America For Its Failure To Comply With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 871.26, Subd. (C) Initial Deposition Of Pmq Witness; Memorandum Of Points And Auth
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-15-2026. LINE 3. PLAINTIFFS SIDA FAGGIOLI, and NATHANIEL FAGGIOLI'S Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America For Its Failure To Comply With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 871.26, Subd. (C) Initial Deposition Of Pmq Witness. Plaintiffs' motion for sanctions against defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America for failure to comply with CCP 871.26(c) is denied. Jaguar has shown good cause within the meaning of CCP 871.26(j) for its failure to produce a PMQ witness within 120 days due to its reasonable request for the entry of a protective order prior to the deposition. See February 9, 2026 order at 2:24-2-3:4 (good cause to seek entry of a protective order before a CCP 871.26 deposition). rulings.Case Title: DAVID MANDELBROT VS. KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-15-2026. LINE 8. 1-DEFENDANTS MELANIE EMMONS, AN INDIVIDUAL, and KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Kato, Feder & Suzuki, LLP and Melanie Emmons' motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is denied as to lines 10-13 on page 15 (damages for trustee fees and estate management fees and trust administration attorneys' fees and costs) and granted as to paragraphs 65 and 73 (successor trustee authority and standing). While Judge Van Aken's January 22, 2026 order did not explicitly provide for additional allegations of damages, nothing in that order suggests that, had the issue been raised with her, she would have prohibited it. Judicial economy strongly favors allowing the plaintiff to allege these additional items of damages without need for a motion to amend absent some substantive deficiency. Defendants' contentions that the additional items of damages are not recoverable is not amenable on a demurrer. Because the additional items of damages are properly alleged, recoverability depends on whether the plaintiff is able to prove entitlement to those items, which is not an issue amenable for resolution on a motion to strike. Judge Moody's rulings do not preclude the plaintiff from recovering these items of damages from defendants. Paragraphs 65 and 73 are stricken because those paragraphs contravene the explicit ruling in Judge Van Aken's January 22, 2026 order that in his capacity as successor trustee plaintiff may not pursue his first and second claims. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. LINE 8. 2-DEFENDANTS MELANIE EMMONS, AN INDIVIDUAL, and KATO, FEDER & SUZUKI, LLP'SEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Kato, Feder & Suzuki, LLP and Melanie Emmons' demurrer to the first and second causes of action for professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty in the first amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. Defendants make four arguments why they believe the first and second causes of action fail to state cognizable claims. rulings.Case Title: CLAUDE PERASSO ET AL VS. TERENCE REDMOND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants David Dell And Coombs & Dunlap Llp?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Wednesday, Apr-15-2026. LINE 6. DEFENDANTS DAVID DELL AN INDIVIDUAL, COOMBS & DUNLAP LLP Defendants David Dell And Coombs & Dunlap LLP's Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C). Defendants David Dell and Coombs & Dunlap LLP's motion for fees and costs pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16(c) is GRANTED. Terence and Melissa Redmond ("Redmond Defendants") own a condominium unit in the Oak Creek East Condominium Project on Silverado Golf Course in Napa, CA. Plaintiffs Claude Perasso, Sharon Bobrow, Roseann Burhenne, and Richard Kramer were HOA Board members (collectively, "the directors") of the Redmond Defendants' HOA in May 2020. The HOA filed a complaint against the Redmonds in September 2020 and obtained a temporary restraining order (TRO) when the Redmonds reconstruction of their home allegedly violated the terms of the HOA. In December 2020, the Redmonds, represented by attorney David Dell and his law firm Coombs & Dunlap, LLP ("the Attorney Defendants") filed a cross-complaint against the HOA and the directors. The case went to trial, and the jury found in favor of the directors. The court issued a statement of decision in favor of the HOA. Judgment was entered on June 10, 2024. ***THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 1:30pm calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
